message.content:
You are reviewing whether an implementation matches its specification.

## What Was Requested

Create `src/utils/cv-html.ts` exporting a single `getCvHtml(): string` function that returns a complete self-contained HTML document with:
- Inline CSS only (no external stylesheets)
- White background, system sans-serif font (ATS-safe, not monospace)
- Professional layout: name, title, contact, then sections: Professional Summary, Core Competencies, Experience, Technical Skills, Education, Languages
- All CV content with quantified metrics (12,000+ orders/day, 40M+ queries/month, 99.99% availability, 40% increase, 20+ engineers, 200+ interviews)
- Print @media rule for A4

## What Implementer Claims They Built

Created src/utils/cv-html.ts with getCvHtml() function. 250 lines. TypeScript compiles. Commit SHA: daeeff7

## Your Job

Read `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/cv-astro/src/utils/cv-html.ts` and verify:
- Exports `getCvHtml(): string`
- Returns a complete HTML document (DOCTYPE, html, head, body)
- Inline CSS only, white background, sans-serif font
- All 6 sections present: Professional Summary, Core Competencies, Experience, Technical Skills, Education, Languages
- All quantified metrics present in Experience section
- Print @media rule present

Report:
- ✅ Spec compliant
- ❌ Issues found: [specific missing items with line references]

content.text: I'll read the implementation file and verify it against the specification.

Review complete. Here is the full verification report.

---

## Verification Report: `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/cv-astro/src/utils/cv-html.ts`

### Function Signature and Structure

- Export: `getCvHtml(): string` is correctly exported as a named export at line 1. The return type is implicitly `string` via the template literal — no explicit `: string` annotation on the return type, but TypeScript infers it correctly. This is a minor style point, not a defect.
- Complete HTML document: DOCTYPE at line 2, `<html lang="en">` at line 3, `<head>` block lines 4–105, `<body>` block lines 106–248. All required structural elements are present.

### CSS Requirements

- Inline CSS only: All styles are inside a `<style>` tag within `<head>`. No `<link>` elements or `@import` rules referencing external resources. Compliant.
- White background: `background: #ffffff` on the `body` rule at line 15. Compliant.
- Sans-serif font (ATS-safe, not monospace): `font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif` at line 11. All fallbacks are sans-serif. No monospace fonts anywhere. Compliant.
- Print @media rule for A4: Lines 100–103 contain `@media print { body { padding: 0; } @page { margin: 1.5cm 2cm; size: A4 portrait; } }`. Compliant.

### Section Presence (h2 headings)

All 6 required sections are present:

- "Professional Summary" — line 117
- "Core Competencies" — line 126
- "Experience" — line 142
- "Technical Skills" — line 232
- "Education" — line 240
- "Languages" — line 245

### Quantified Metrics in Experience Section

All 6 required metrics are present:

- `12,000+ orders/day` — lines 120 and 151
- `40M+ queries/month` — lines 121 and 152
- `99.99% availability` — lines 121 and 152
- `40% increase` — line 153 ("40% increase in recommendation-attributed purchases")
- `20+ engineers` — line 155
- `200+ interviews` — line 155

### Professional Layout

Header presents name (line 108), title (line 109), and contact block (lines 110–115) in the correct order before sections begin. Layout is logical and professional.

---

## Verdict

Spec compliant. All specified requirements are met:

- `getCvHtml(): string` exported correctly
- Complete HTML document with DOCTYPE, html, head, body
- Inline CSS only, white background, ATS-safe sans-serif font stack
- All 6 sections present in the specified order
- All 6 quantified metrics present in the Experience section
- Print `@media` rule with A4 page size present

No critical or important issues found. One minor observation: the return type annotation could be made explicit (`): string {`) for stricter TypeScript style, but this is a suggestion only and does not affect correctness or spec compliance.
